title: Bound memory with TTL and an eviction policy
description: Set maxmemory with an appropriate eviction policy and TTLs so the keyspace cannot grow until Redis OOMs.
body: Use when keys are created continuously (sessions, cache entries). Do not run with no maxmemory or with noeviction on a pure cache, which lets memory grow until writes fail or the process is killed. Actions: set maxmemory to fit the instance, choose an eviction policy that matches usage (allkeys-lru/allkeys-lfu for a cache, volatile-* if some keys must persist), and give cache entries a TTL. Validation signals include a test under load showing memory plateaus at maxmemory and least-valuable keys are evicted rather than writes failing. Risk boundary: noeviction on a growing cache turns a memory spike into an outage; if some keys must never be evicted, isolate them on a separate instance and flag the mixing.
